commands.review_summary:
The branch changes heartbeat wake classification so background-task completion wakes run on the owning session despite heartbeat isolation, and changes subagent announce prompts to mark required progress-only completions as blocked.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +92. Total +113 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main queues background-task events on the owner session but does not classify those wakes as same-queue wake payloads, while isolated heartbeat runs use a separate :heartbeat session. I did not run a live Telegram/gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor or maintainer validation, not an automated code repair: add real behavior proof and get CI green before merge.

target.title: fix: deliver background task completions through owning sessions
target.body:
## Summary
- Deliver background-task completion wake events through the owning session even when heartbeat isolation is enabled.
- Treat required subagent completion handoffs with progress-only output as blocked instead of successful.
- Add focused regression coverage for both paths.

## Problem
Background task completion events can be queued on the real requester session, such as a Telegram topic session, while heartbeat isolation runs the wake in a separate heartbeat session. That can leave the user without a visible final/blocker message even though the task finished. Separately, required subagent completions could present progress-only text as a successful handoff.

## Fix
The heartbeat runner now classifies `background-task` and `background-task-blocked` wake payloads as real wake events, inspects pending session events for them, and avoids heartbeat isolation only for that delivery path. Subagent announce formatting now uses the existing required-completion terminal classifier so progress-only output becomes a blocked handoff.

## OpenClaw guard/gate review
- AGENTS / nested AGENTS checked: yes, source checkout instructions and OpenClaw upstream PR standard were reviewed.
- Native schema/config/API contract checked: yes, this uses existing heartbeat wake sources and task completion contract paths.
- Runtime/session/message boundary checked: yes, risk surface is task completion delivery into requester sessions, especially Telegram topic sessions.
- Upgrade/backward compatibility checked: yes, no config changes; normal heartbeat isolation remains unchanged except for background-task completion wake delivery.

## Proof
### Automated
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.capture-completion-reply.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`
- Passed: 3 shards, 8 test files, 258 tests total.

### Build
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch`
- Passed: runtime build path, CLI bootstrap import guard, runtime postbuild, build stamp, runtime postbuild stamp.

## Risks and counterexamples
- Heartbeat isolation remains enabled for ordinary heartbeat wakes.
- Background-task wake delivery uses the owning session only for the completion event path.
- Progress-only required completion output is blocked, while normal successful worker output remains successful.

## Remaining gaps
- Upstream CI has not run yet.
- Full `npm run build` was not used for the final refresh because the first attempt stalled in plugin/UI dependency download work outside this four-file runtime change.
- No live gateway restart or Telegram smoke was performed as part of this PR refresh.
